label: work
title: Abdominal imaging report
content:
HISTORY:  Post-prandial right upper quadrant pain.  Evaluate for
cholelithiasis.

No priors are available.

ABDOMINAL ULTRASOUND:

The liver parenchyma is homogenous with no focal lesions identified.  The
portal vein is patent with normal hepatopetal flow.  The gallbladder is normal
and displays no evidence of wall thickening or cholelithiasis.  No
intrahepatic ductal dilatation is present with the CHD measuring 3 mm.
Pancreas is unremarkable and the aorta is of normal caliber throughout.  The
spleen is slightly prominent measuring just under 11.3 cm.  No evidence of
hydronephrosis or renal calculi is identified bilaterally.  Limited evaluation
of the urinary bladder is unremarkable.  Incidentally detected is a simple-
appearing 4 x 5 x 4.3 cm cystic lesion in the region of the right adnexa.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Normal abdominal ultrasound without evidence of cholelithiasis.

2.  Slightly prominent splenic size of uncertain clinical significance.

3.  Incidentally detected right adnexal simple-appearing cyst.  Given its
size, a six-week followup pelvic ultrasound is recommended to document
resolution.
